title: Deterministic headless frontend interaction harness
summary: Tests can drive real Neovim cursor and mode transitions, reconstruct displayed frontend text, and verify source integrity without a GUI.
details: tests/support/frontend_harness.lua opens scratch C, C++, or CUDA buffers through the real FileType and frontend autocmd pipeline.
The harness enters characterwise, linewise, and blockwise Visual modes using real Normal/input commands and waits for scheduled redraw work.
Display reconstruction applies ds_* extmark conceal, inline virtual text, and overlay virtual text to source rows.
tools/run_tests.py runs each spec in an isolated Neovim process and converts printed failure summaries, editor errors, timeouts, and process exits into one reliable exit status.
Outputs are ephemeral because the entire isolated suite is computationally cheap and reproducible.
rationale: Static render functions cannot validate mode/event ordering, stale namespace state, cursor reveal, selection growth, or asynchronous repaint behavior.
Isolation prevents module globals, augroups, buffers, and window matches from leaking between specs.

provenance.note: Owner required an agent-friendly way to experiment with real frontend interactions.
Current harness and isolated runner implementation.

evidence.claim: Nineteen isolated specs currently complete in approximately seven seconds on the development host.

questions: How can an agent reproduce a cursor or selection interaction without Neovide?
Which command reliably runs every frontend test?
Are test result logs durable artifacts?

last_verified.method: All isolated specs executed through the new runner.
